page: Amazon Web Services i7ie.6xlarge is a storage-optimized server powered by an Intel Xeon 8559C processor built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 24 dedicated vCPUs across 12 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. For storage-heavy workloads, it bundles 15000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The network interface delivers a baseline bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong capabilities in memory and data processing. The server achieved a top-tier Geekbench single-core score of 2314.0 and a strong multi-core score of 16504.0. Its memory subsystem demonstrates strong write performance with a large block write bandwidth of 107.16 GB/sec, alongside a strong Passmark memory score of 2868.29. Single-threaded text processing and small-model LLM text generation both rank in the top tier. Decompression and compression tasks also show strong performance, with bzip3 multi-threaded decompression reaching 168.6 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the i7ie.6xlarge offers high resource density by combining a large 15000 GB NVMe SSD storage capacity with a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. This makes it highly efficient for workloads requiring massive local storage and high memory capacity without the need for external block storage. However, the baseline network bandwidth is limited to 12 Gbps, which may act as a bottleneck for highly distributed network-bound tasks. This server is best suited for large-scale databases, data warehousing, high-throughput web serving, and memory-intensive data processing applications.
description: Amazon Web Services i7ie.6xlarge is a storage-optimized server featuring 24 dedicated vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 8559C processor and 192.0 GB of DDR5 memory. It is equipped with 15000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a 12 Gbps baseline network interface. Benchmark results show top-tier performance in single-core Geekbench tests and text processing, alongside strong multi-core capabilities and large-block memory write speeds. The server's high storage-to-compute ratio and DDR5 memory architecture deliver excellent resource density for data-intensive applications. It is highly suited for large databases, data warehousing, and high-throughput web serving, though its 12 Gbps network bandwidth represents a potential tradeoff for network-heavy distributed workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i7ie.6xlarge is a storage-optimized server with 24 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR5 RAM, and 15000 GB local NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier single-core and text processing performance.

tagline: A storage-optimized platform combining massive local NVMe SSD capacity with high-speed DDR5 memory for demanding database and data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 24 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 192 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory.
15000 GB of high-performance local NVMe SSD storage.
Top-tier Geekbench single-core and text processing benchmark results.
Strong memory write bandwidth of 107.16 GB/sec.
Optimized for large-scale databases, data warehousing, and web serving.
